raw_cv_text:
Aleksandr Kowalski
+44 20 7946 0958 | Email: aleksandr.kowalski@gmail.com | LinkedIn: aleksandr-kowalski | GitHub: AleksandrKowalski |
https://aleksandr.dev
EDUCATION
ETH Zurich (Swiss Federal Institute of Technology) | MSc Computer Science 2010–2012
Thesis on distributed systems optimization achieved 9.2/10 (top 3% cohort); published 2 peer-
reviewed papers on lock-free concurrent data structures in IEEE Transactions on Parallel and
Distributed Systems
Led Systems Engineering Club with 150+ members; organized 8 technical workshops on concurrency
patterns, attracting speakers from Google and Microsoft
University of Warsaw | BSc Mathematics & Computer Science 2006–2010
Graduated summa cum laude with 3.95/4.0 GPA; awarded Faculty Excellence Award for
contributions to algorithmic research
Conducted undergraduate research on randomized algorithms under Prof. Marek Cygan; co-authored
1 publication in competitive programming domain
EXPERIENCE
Stripe | Principal Engineer, Infrastructure & Performance 2019–Present
Architected and led 12-person team delivering next-generation payment processing pipeline; reduced
P99 latency by 340ms (from 450ms→110ms) and increased throughput 2.8x (180K→504K req/sec)
serving $2.2T+ annual payment volume
Designed distributed consensus protocol for real-time settlement reconciliation across 18 global
regions; achieved 99.9997% consistency (3 nines improvement) while cutting operational overhead
by $4.2M annually
Mentored 5 senior engineers to principal-track roles; established incident command training program
reducing MTTR by 62% and incident severity by 58% across 200+ microservices
Cloudflare | Senior Staff Engineer, Network Systems 2016–2019
Built HTTP/3 and QUIC optimization layer deployed across 280+ edge locations processing 40M
req/sec; improved median latency 230ms→85ms (63% reduction) and reduced packet loss recovery
overhead by 45%
Led platform reliability initiative improving global DNS resolution SLA from 99.95%→99.999% (4-
nines improvement); saved customers ~$18M in potential downtime costs annually
Championed adoption of eBPF-based network telemetry across infrastructure; cut observability costs
by 67% ($2.8M savings) while increasing metric resolution from 60s to 1s intervals
Shopify | Staff Engineer, Platform & Databases 2013–2016
Redesigned distributed sharding architecture for primary transactional database (900GB+, 250K
queries/sec); achieved 4x improvement in query latency (from 280ms→70ms) and enabled 5.2x
capacity scaling without hardware upgrades
Implemented real-time replication system for cross-datacenter failover; reduced RTO from 45
minutes→4 minutes and RPO from 30s→<100ms, earning $6.8M in SLA credit avoidance
Managed platform infrastructure for 850K+ merchant stores; optimized resource allocation reducing
cloud spend by $3.1M YoY while maintaining 99.98% uptime SLA across containerized workloads
Google | Senior Software Engineer, Infrastructure 2011–2013
Developed high-performance RPC framework for MapReduce clusters processing 8 exabytes/day; cut
request serialization overhead by 73% through custom binary protocol and reduced tail latency (p99)
from 320ms→80ms
Owned cluster resource scheduler serving 14K compute nodes across 6 datacenters; improved
resource utilization by 31% ($12M annual savings) and reduced job startup time by 52%
Co-authored 2 internal papers on distributed tracing and adaptive batching; knowledge transferred to
28 engineers across 4 teams reducing incident resolution time by 44%
PROJECTS
CacheMeld | Rust, C++, Redis, Memcached 2020–Present
Open-source distributed cache coherence framework with intelligent multi-tier invalidation; 4,200+
GitHub stars, 187K monthly npm downloads, and adopted by 40+ companies including 2 Fortune
500s
Achieved 95th percentile cache hit rate 87.4% on real-world workloads vs. 71% for competitors;
provided 4.2x memory efficiency through adaptive compression, reducing production cache footprint
by $800K annually for users
VectorFlow | Go, Python, TensorFlow, Protocol Buffers 2018–2022
Machine learning inference serving platform with dynamic batching; achieved 8,600+ GitHub stars
and integrated into 12 production systems at Stripe, Shopify, and Figma
Delivered 6.8x throughput improvement (from 420→2,856 inferences/sec per GPU) through model
quantization and batch optimization; reduced inference latency from 142ms→38ms while cutting
GPU costs by 58%
RaftControl | Go, Protobuf, etcd, Kubernetes 2019–2021
Consensus algorithm testing and verification framework; 2,100+ GitHub stars with adoption in 18
production systems; featured in 3 academic papers on consensus correctness
Reduced consensus failure detection time from 14s→1.2s through improved leader election logic;
generated 500K+ test scenarios covering edge cases, catching 47 previously unknown bugs in popular
implementations
EXTRACURRICULAR
Strange Loop Conference & QCon | Technical Speaker & Program Committee Member 2017–Present
Delivered 6 keynotes and technical talks on distributed systems, concurrency, and performance
optimization with combined audience reach of 8,400+ engineers; talks achieved 4.7/5.0 average
speaker rating
Served on program committee for 4 conferences selecting 180+ talks from 2,100+ submissions;
mentored 12 first-time speakers resulting in 9 accepted presentations and fostering emerging voices in
systems community
Open Source Systems Mentorship Initiative | Principal Mentor & Advisor 2016–Present
Actively mentor 7 open-source projects across Rust ecosystem; 4 mentees promoted to core
maintainer roles within 18 months; mentored codebase now maintains 99.2% test coverage with zero
critical CVEs in 3-year period
Authored comprehensive distributed systems and performance optimization guide (100K+ GitHub
clones, 340K readers); established weekly office hours attended by 45+ engineers, providing
technical guidance on architecture decisions and code reviews
TECHNICAL SKILLS
Languages – Go, Rust, C++, Python, Java, SQL, Bash, JavaScript
Technologies – Kubernetes, Distributed Systems, Consensus Algorithms (Raft, Paxos), Protocol Buffers,
gRPC, REST APIs, Event Streaming (Kafka), PostgreSQL, DynamoDB, Redis, Message Queues
(RabbitMQ), TensorFlow, Docker, Terraform, etcd, Datadog, Prometheus
Tools – Git, Linux, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, CloudFormation, GitHub Actions, Jenkins,
Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, PagerDuty, VirtualBox, GCP, AWS, Azure, Bazel
